# Reading, querying and writing GWAS summary data in VCF format

Complete GWAS summary datasets are now abundant. A large repository of
curated, harmonised and QC’d datasets is available in the [IEU GWAS
database](https://gwas.mrcieu.ac.uk/). They can be queried via the
[API](https://api.opengwas.io/api/) directly, or through the
[ieugwasr](https://github.com/mrcieu/ieugwasr) R package, or the
[ieugwaspy](https://github.com/mrcieu/ieugwaspy) Python package.
However, for faster querying that can be used in a HPC environment,
accessing the data directly and not through cloud systems is
advantageous.

We developed a format for storing and harmonising GWAS summary data
known as [GWAS VCF
format](https://github.com/MRCIEU/gwas-vcf-specification/releases/tag/1.0.0)
which can be created using
[gwas2vcf](https://github.com/mrcieu/gwas2vcf). All the data in the [IEU
GWAS database](https://gwas.mrcieu.ac.uk/) is available for download in
this format. This R package provides fast and convenient functions for
querying and creating GWAS summary data in GWAS VCF format (v1.0). See
also [pygwasvcf](https://github.com/mrcieu/pygwasvcf) a Python3 parser
for querying GWAS VCF files.

This package includes:

- a wrapper around the
  [bioconductor/VariantAnnotation](https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/html/VariantAnnotation.html)
  package, providing functions tailored to GWAS VCF for reading,
  querying, creating and writing GWAS VCF format files
- some LD related functions such as using a reference panel to extract
  proxies, create LD matrices and perform LD clumping
- functions for harmonising a dataset against the reference genome and
  creating GWAS VCF files.

See also the [gwasglue](https://github.com/MRCIEU/gwasglue) R package
for methods to connect the VCF data to Mendelian randomization,
colocalisation, fine mapping etc.

## Installation

You can install a binary version from our [MRC IEU
r-universe](https://mrcieu.r-universe.dev/builds) with

``` r
install.packages('gwasvcf', repos = c('https://mrcieu.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org'))
```

or install from the GitHub repo

``` r
remotes::install_github("mrcieu/gwasvcf")
```

## Usage

See vignettes here: <https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/>.

## Citation

If using GWAS-VCF files please reference the studies that you use and
the following paper:

**The variant call format provides efficient and robust storage of GWAS
summary statistics.** Matthew Lyon, Shea J Andrews, Ben Elsworth, Tom R
Gaunt, Gibran Hemani, Edoardo Marcora. bioRxiv 2020.05.29.115824; doi:
<https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.05.29.115824>

## Reference datasets

Example GWAS VCF (GIANT 2010 BMI):

- <http://fileserve.mrcieu.ac.uk/vcf/IEU-a-2.vcf.gz>
- <http://fileserve.mrcieu.ac.uk/vcf/IEU-a-2.vcf.gz.tbi>

1000 genomes reference panels for LD for each superpopulation - used by
default in OpenGWAS:

- <http://fileserve.mrcieu.ac.uk/ld/1kg.v3.tgz>

RSID index for faster querying:

- <http://fileserve.mrcieu.ac.uk/vcf/annotations.vcf.gz.rsidx>

1000 genomes annotations in vcf format harmonised against human genome
reference:

- <http://fileserve.mrcieu.ac.uk/vcf/1kg_v3_nomult.vcf.gz>
- <http://fileserve.mrcieu.ac.uk/vcf/1kg_v3_nomult.vcf.gz.tbi>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Notes

#### Example data

data.vcf.gz and data.vcf.gz.tbi are the first few rows of the Speliotes
2010 BMI GWAS

The eur.bed/bim/fam files are the same range as data.vcf.gz, from here
<http://fileserve.mrcieu.ac.uk/ld/data_maf0.01_rs_ref.tgz>

# Package index

## All functions

- [`VariantAnnotation`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/VariantAnnotation.md)
  : VariantAnnotation
- [`check_bcftools()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/check_bcftools.md)
  : Check if the tools_bcftools option is set
- [`check_plink()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/check_plink.md)
  : Check if the tools_plink option is set
- [`create_ldref_sqlite()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/create_ldref_sqlite.md)
  : Create LD reference sqlite database for tags
- [`create_pval_index_from_vcf()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/create_pval_index_from_vcf.md)
  : Create pval index from GWAS-VCF file
- [`create_rsidx_index_from_vcf()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/create_rsidx_index_from_vcf.md)
  : Create RSID index from VCF
- [`create_rsidx_sub_index()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/create_rsidx_sub_index.md)
  : Create new index from existing index using a subset of rsids
- [`create_vcf()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/create_vcf.md)
  : Create GWAS vcf
- [`get_ld_proxies()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/get_ld_proxies.md)
  : Find LD proxies for a set of SNPs
- [`gwasvcf_to_summaryset()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/gwasvcf_to_summaryset.md)
  : Create a SummarySet
- [`merge_vcf()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/merge_vcf.md)
  : Merge two GWAS VCF objects
- [`parse_chrompos()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/parse_chrompos.md)
  : Parse chromosome:position
- [`proxy_match()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/proxy_match.md)
  : Extract SNPs from vcf file
- [`query_chrompos_bcftools()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_chrompos_bcftools.md)
  : Query chromosome and position using bcftools
- [`query_chrompos_file()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_chrompos_file.md)
  : Query vcf file, extracting by chromosome and position
- [`query_chrompos_vcf()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_chrompos_vcf.md)
  : Query chrompos from vcf object
- [`query_gwas()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_gwas.md)
  : Query data from vcf file
- [`query_pval_bcftools()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_pval_bcftools.md)
  : Query p-value using bcftools
- [`query_pval_file()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_pval_file.md)
  : Query pval from vcf file
- [`query_pval_sqlite3()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_pval_sqlite3.md)
  : Query pval from file using pvali index
- [`query_pval_vcf()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_pval_vcf.md)
  : Query based on p-value threshold from vcf
- [`query_pvali()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_pvali.md)
  : Query pvali
- [`query_rsid_bcftools()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_rsid_bcftools.md)
  : Query
- [`query_rsid_file()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_rsid_file.md)
  : Query vcf file, extracting by rsid
- [`query_rsid_rsidx()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_rsid_rsidx.md)
  : Query rsid from file using rsidx index
- [`query_rsid_vcf()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_rsid_vcf.md)
  : Query rsid from vcf object
- [`query_rsidx()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/query_rsidx.md)
  : Query rsidx
- [`set_bcftools()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/set_bcftools.md)
  : Set bcftools binary location
- [`set_plink()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/set_plink.md)
  : Set plink binary location
- [`sqlite_ld_proxies()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/sqlite_ld_proxies.md)
  : Lookup LD proxies from sqlite database
- [`vcf_to_granges()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/vcf_to_granges.md)
  : Convert vcf format to granges format
- [`vcf_to_tibble()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/vcf_to_tibble.md)
  : Convert vcf format to tibble (data frame)
- [`vcflist_overlaps()`](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/reference/vcflist_overlaps.md)
  : Reduce list of VCFs to intersecting regions

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Reading, querying and writing GWAS summary data in VCF
  format](https://mrcieu.github.io/gwasvcf/articles/guide.md):
